content.content:
1→# Plan: Fix All Audit Issues — Orchestrator Hardening + Scraper Fix
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→A comprehensive audit of the Mira AI agent revealed 14 issues across the orchestrator, prompt engineering, data pipeline, and Socket.io layer. This plan addresses all of them. The scraper fix will be delegated to a separate parallel agent since it touches a different part of the codebase.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Part A: Orchestrator Fixes (main agent)
    10→
    11→### A1. Enforce API call limit — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`
    12→
    13→**Problem:** `SOFT_API_LIMIT = 20` (line 34) is defined but never checked. `_call_claude` ↔ `_handle_tool_calls` can recurse infinitely.
    14→
    15→**Fix:** Add a guard at the top of `_call_claude()` (line 184):
    16→```python
    17→if session.api_calls >= SOFT_API_LIMIT:
    18→    print(f"[mira] API limit reached for {session.user_id}")
    19→    return
    20→```
    21→
    22→### A2. Concurrency guard — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`
    23→
    24→**Problem:** Silence timer fires during active Claude calls, causing parallel mutations to `conversation_history`.
    25→
    26→**Fix:**
    27→- Add `is_processing: bool = False` field to `SessionState` (line 51 area)
    28→- Set `is_processing = True` at top of `handle_event()` (line 137), reset to `False` at end
    29→- In `_silence_callback()` (line 403), check `session.is_processing` before calling `handle_event()`
    30→- Wrap `handle_event` body in try/finally to always reset the flag
    31→
    32→### A3. Error handling in `_call_claude` — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`
    33→
    34→**Problem:** No try/except around Claude API call (line 192). Failures leave conversation history in invalid state.
    35→
    36→**Fix:** Wrap the streaming block in try/except. On failure, pop the last user message from history to keep it consistent, log the error.
    37→
    38→### A4. Error handling in `_handle_tool_calls` — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`
    39→
    40→**Problem:** `execute_tool()` can raise (Serper `raise_for_status` at `tools.py:112`). Inconsistent with `search_gmail` which catches internally.
    41→
    42→**Fix:** Wrap `execute_tool()` call in try/except inside `_handle_tool_calls()`. On failure, return an error dict as the tool result so Claude can handle it gracefully.
    43→
    44→### A5. Wrap `_generate_summary` in try/except — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`
    45→
    46→**Problem:** `_generate_summary()` at line 281 is outside the try/except. Claude API failure prevents session cleanup.
    47→
    48→**Fix:** Wrap in try/except, fall back to a generic summary string like `"Session ended before summary could be generated."`.
    49→
    50→### A6. Disconnect cleanup + sid-to-user mapping — `backend/main.py`
    51→
    52→**Problem:** Disconnect handler (line 51) does nothing. Orphaned sessions accumulate.
    53→
    54→**Fix:**
    55→- Add `_sid_to_user: dict[str, str] = {}` at module level
    56→- In `join_room`, store the mapping: `_sid_to_user[sid] = user_id`
    57→- In `disconnect`, look up `user_id = _sid_to_user.pop(sid, None)`, then call `mira.end_session(user_id)` if there's an active session
    58→- Also add try/except around `mirror_event` handler (line 84-90)
    59→
    60→### A7. Fix CORS mismatch — `backend/main.py`
    61→
    62→**Problem:** `cors_allowed_origins=[]` on Socket.io server (line 12) rejects WebSocket upgrades in production.
    63→
    64→**Fix:** Change to `cors_allowed_origins="*"` (or pass the frontend URL from env).
    65→
    66→### A8. Session deduplication — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`
    67→
    68→**Problem:** Double `start_session` call orphans the first session's timers.
    69→
    70→**Fix:** At top of `start_session()`, check if `user_id` already has an active session. If so, end it first:
    71→```python
    72→if user_id in self.sessions:
    73→    await self.end_session(user_id)
    74→```
    75→
    76→---
    77→
    78→## Part B: Prompt & Memory Fixes (main agent)
    79→
    80→### B1. Empty purchase history fallback — `backend/agent/prompts.py`
    81→
    82→**Problem:** If `_filter_fashion_purchases()` returns empty, Mira's opening (roast a specific purchase) has no data.
    83→
    84→**Fix:** After the `## Recent Purchases` section in `build_system_prompt`, if `filtered_purchases` is empty, add:
    85→```
    86→## Recent Purchases
    87→No purchase history available. Skip the purchase roast — instead, open by commenting on what you can see (their outfit, their vibe) and ask about their style preferences directly.
    88→```
    89→
    90→### B2. Include liked items from past sessions — `backend/agent/prompts.py` + `memory.py`
    91→
    92→**Problem:** `load_past_sessions` returns full `outfit_data` dicts (which include `liked_items`), but `build_system_prompt` only uses `summary`.
    93→
    94→**Fix:** In `build_system_prompt`, also extract `liked_items` from each past session dict:
    95→```python
    96→for session in session_history[-3:]:
    97→    parts.append(f"- {session.get('summary', 'No summary')}")
    98→    liked = session.get("liked_items", [])
    99→    if liked:
   100→        names = [item.get("title", "?") for item in liked[:3]]
   101→        parts.append(f"  Liked: {', '.join(names)}")
   102→```
   103→
   104→### B3. Remove silence double-prompting — `backend/agent/prompts.py`
   105→
   106→**Problem:** Personality says "be proactive if silent" AND the silence event sends an explicit command.
   107→
   108→**Fix:** Remove the proactive silence instruction from `MIRA_PERSONALITY` line 14 (the silence timer event in `_event_to_message` already handles this at line 381-383).
   109→
   110→### B4. Increase purchase fetch limit — `backend/agent/memory.py`
   111→
   112→**Problem:** DB fetches 30 purchases, prompt filter discards many, leaving fewer than 20 fashion items.
   113→
   114→**Fix:** Change `LIMIT 30` to `LIMIT 50` in `load_user_purchases` (line 102) to provide more buffer for the filter.
   115→
   116→---
   117→
   118→## Part C: DB Query Optimization (main agent)
   119→
   120→### C1. Parallelize session-start DB calls — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`
   121→
   122→**Problem:** 5 sequential HTTP round trips to Neon at session start.
   123→
   124→**Fix:** Use `asyncio.gather()`:
   125→```python
   126→profile, purchases, past_sessions, oauth_token = await asyncio.gather(
   127→    load_user_profile(db, user_id),
   128→    load_user_purchases(db, user_id),
   129→    load_past_sessions(db, user_id),
   130→    get_user_oauth_token(db, user_id),
   131→)
   132→```
   133→
   134→### C2. Merge profile queries into JOIN — `backend/agent/memory.py`
   135→
   136→**Problem:** `load_user_profile` makes 2 sequential HTTP calls (users + style_profiles).
   137→

file.content:
# Plan: Fix All Audit Issues — Orchestrator Hardening + Scraper Fix

## Context

A comprehensive audit of the Mira AI agent revealed 14 issues across the orchestrator, prompt engineering, data pipeline, and Socket.io layer. This plan addresses all of them. The scraper fix will be delegated to a separate parallel agent since it touches a different part of the codebase.

---

## Part A: Orchestrator Fixes (main agent)

### A1. Enforce API call limit — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Problem:** `SOFT_API_LIMIT = 20` (line 34) is defined but never checked. `_call_claude` ↔ `_handle_tool_calls` can recurse infinitely.

**Fix:** Add a guard at the top of `_call_claude()` (line 184):
```python
if session.api_calls >= SOFT_API_LIMIT:
    print(f"[mira] API limit reached for {session.user_id}")
    return
```

### A2. Concurrency guard — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Problem:** Silence timer fires during active Claude calls, causing parallel mutations to `conversation_history`.

**Fix:**
- Add `is_processing: bool = False` field to `SessionState` (line 51 area)
- Set `is_processing = True` at top of `handle_event()` (line 137), reset to `False` at end
- In `_silence_callback()` (line 403), check `session.is_processing` before calling `handle_event()`
- Wrap `handle_event` body in try/finally to always reset the flag

### A3. Error handling in `_call_claude` — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Problem:** No try/except around Claude API call (line 192). Failures leave conversation history in invalid state.

**Fix:** Wrap the streaming block in try/except. On failure, pop the last user message from history to keep it consistent, log the error.

### A4. Error handling in `_handle_tool_calls` — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Problem:** `execute_tool()` can raise (Serper `raise_for_status` at `tools.py:112`). Inconsistent with `search_gmail` which catches internally.

**Fix:** Wrap `execute_tool()` call in try/except inside `_handle_tool_calls()`. On failure, return an error dict as the tool result so Claude can handle it gracefully.

### A5. Wrap `_generate_summary` in try/except — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Problem:** `_generate_summary()` at line 281 is outside the try/except. Claude API failure prevents session cleanup.

**Fix:** Wrap in try/except, fall back to a generic summary string like `"Session ended before summary could be generated."`.

### A6. Disconnect cleanup + sid-to-user mapping — `backend/main.py`

**Problem:** Disconnect handler (line 51) does nothing. Orphaned sessions accumulate.

**Fix:**
- Add `_sid_to_user: dict[str, str] = {}` at module level
- In `join_room`, store the mapping: `_sid_to_user[sid] = user_id`
- In `disconnect`, look up `user_id = _sid_to_user.pop(sid, None)`, then call `mira.end_session(user_id)` if there's an active session
- Also add try/except around `mirror_event` handler (line 84-90)

### A7. Fix CORS mismatch — `backend/main.py`

**Problem:** `cors_allowed_origins=[]` on Socket.io server (line 12) rejects WebSocket upgrades in production.

**Fix:** Change to `cors_allowed_origins="*"` (or pass the frontend URL from env).

### A8. Session deduplication — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Problem:** Double `start_session` call orphans the first session's timers.

**Fix:** At top of `start_session()`, check if `user_id` already has an active session. If so, end it first:
```python
if user_id in self.sessions:
    await self.end_session(user_id)
```

---

## Part B: Prompt & Memory Fixes (main agent)

### B1. Empty purchase history fallback — `backend/agent/prompts.py`

**Problem:** If `_filter_fashion_purchases()` returns empty, Mira's opening (roast a specific purchase) has no data.

**Fix:** After the `## Recent Purchases` section in `build_system_prompt`, if `filtered_purchases` is empty, add:
```
## Recent Purchases
No purchase history available. Skip the purchase roast — instead, open by commenting on what you can see (their outfit, their vibe) and ask about their style preferences directly.
```

### B2. Include liked items from past sessions — `backend/agent/prompts.py` + `memory.py`

**Problem:** `load_past_sessions` returns full `outfit_data` dicts (which include `liked_items`), but `build_system_prompt` only uses `summary`.

**Fix:** In `build_system_prompt`, also extract `liked_items` from each past session dict:
```python
for session in session_history[-3:]:
    parts.append(f"- {session.get('summary', 'No summary')}")
    liked = session.get("liked_items", [])
    if liked:
        names = [item.get("title", "?") for item in liked[:3]]
        parts.append(f"  Liked: {', '.join(names)}")
```

### B3. Remove silence double-prompting — `backend/agent/prompts.py`

**Problem:** Personality says "be proactive if silent" AND the silence event sends an explicit command.

**Fix:** Remove the proactive silence instruction from `MIRA_PERSONALITY` line 14 (the silence timer event in `_event_to_message` already handles this at line 381-383).

### B4. Increase purchase fetch limit — `backend/agent/memory.py`

**Problem:** DB fetches 30 purchases, prompt filter discards many, leaving fewer than 20 fashion items.

**Fix:** Change `LIMIT 30` to `LIMIT 50` in `load_user_purchases` (line 102) to provide more buffer for the filter.

---

## Part C: DB Query Optimization (main agent)

### C1. Parallelize session-start DB calls — `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**Problem:** 5 sequential HTTP round trips to Neon at session start.

**Fix:** Use `asyncio.gather()`:
```python
profile, purchases, past_sessions, oauth_token = await asyncio.gather(
    load_user_profile(db, user_id),
    load_user_purchases(db, user_id),
    load_past_sessions(db, user_id),
    get_user_oauth_token(db, user_id),
)
```

### C2. Merge profile queries into JOIN — `backend/agent/memory.py`

**Problem:** `load_user_profile` makes 2 sequential HTTP calls (users + style_profiles).

**Fix:** Merge into one query:
```sql
SELECT u.id, u.name, u.email,
       sp.brands, sp.price_range, sp.style_tags, sp.size_info, sp.narrative_summary
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN style_profiles sp ON sp.user_id = u.id
WHERE u.id = $1
```

---

## Part D: Tool Hardening (main agent)

### D1. Add try/except to `_search_clothing` — `backend/agent/tools.py`

**Problem:** `response.raise_for_status()` at line 112 crashes the tool loop.

**Fix:** Wrap in try/except like `_search_gmail` already does:
```python
try:
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        ...
except Exception as e:
    return {"error": f"Shopping search failed: {str(e)}", "results": []}
```

---

## Part E: Scraper Fix (separate parallel agent)

The scraper agent will fix the root causes of noisy purchase data in `backend/scraper/purchase_parser.py`:

1. **Tighten `_is_receipt()`**: Change from OR to AND logic — require BOTH sender match AND subject keyword match. Remove `"noreply@"` from `RECEIPT_SENDERS`.
2. **Fix subject-line fallback**: Require a price to be present before storing a subject as an item. Reject items where `item_name` has no fashion-related keywords.
3. **Fix `_categorize_item()`**: Add exclusion keywords so "short selling" doesn't become "bottoms" and "top picks" doesn't become "tops".
4. **Rebuild style profiles**: After fixing the parser, apply `_filter_fashion_purchases` logic at the `profile_builder.py` level too, so `build_style_profile()` only considers fashion items for brands/price_range.

---

## Files Summary

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `backend/agent/orchestrator.py` | A1 (API limit), A2 (concurrency guard), A3 (Claude error handling), A4 (tool error handling), A5 (summary error handling), A8 (dedup), C1 (parallel DB) |
| `backend/main.py` | A6 (disconnect cleanup + sid mapping), A7 (CORS fix), add try/except to mirror_event |
| `backend/agent/prompts.py` | B1 (empty purchase fallback), B2 (liked items from past sessions), B3 (remove silence double-prompt) |
| `backend/agent/memory.py` | B4 (increase LIMIT to 50), C2 (merge profile into JOIN query) |
| `backend/agent/tools.py` | D1 (try/except on search_clothing) |
| `backend/scraper/purchase_parser.py` | E1-E3 (separate agent: receipt gate, subject fallback, categorizer) |
| `backend/scraper/profile_builder.py` | E4 (separate agent: filter before profile build) |

## Verification

1. Start backend + frontend, open `/chat`
2. Start session — verify no crashes, Mira responds
3. Send multiple messages rapidly — verify silence timer doesn't cause duplicate responses
4. Check debug system prompt — verify empty purchase fallback text appears for users without fashion purchases
5. Disconnect client mid-session — verify session is cleaned up (check server logs)
6. Test with demo user (no purchases) — verify Mira gracefully handles missing data
7. After scraper fix: re-scrape a user, verify `purchases` table has cleaner data

Based on the search results, I found information about Poke and The Interaction Company, though the specific topic of "purchase history" appears in a technical context rather than as a direct product feature.

Purchase histories document life transitions—from apartment essentials to baby supplies, according to a technical analysis of Poke's architecture. This reference appears in the context of treating email access as memory infrastructure, as your inbox is a living archive of your life.

More broadly, Poke is an iMessage-native AI assistant that provides actionable nudges on the numerous action items we juggle in our everyday lives. Poke claims to connect to your email, calendar, and files, then acts on your behalf through short text threads, drafting replies, rescheduling meetings, paying invoices, booking travel, and more.

The purchase history reference appears to be part of the broader data that Poke can access and analyze from users' emails and digital activity to provide context-aware assistance, rather than a standalone feature specifically called "purchase history."

1→-- 001_initial_schema.sql
     2→-- Creates all tables for the Mirrorless application
     3→
     4→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgcrypto";
     5→
     6→CREATE TABLE users (
     7→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     8→  name text NOT NULL,
     9→  email text UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    10→  phone text,
    11→  google_oauth_token jsonb,
    12→  poke_id text,
    13→  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    14→);
    15→
    16→CREATE TABLE style_profiles (
    17→  user_id uuid PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    18→  brands text[] DEFAULT '{}',
    19→  price_range jsonb,
    20→  style_tags text[] DEFAULT '{}',
    21→  size_info jsonb,
    22→  narrative_summary text
    23→);
    24→
    25→CREATE TABLE purchases (
    26→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    27→  user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    28→  brand text NOT NULL,
    29→  item_name text NOT NULL,
    30→  category text,
    31→  price numeric,
    32→  date date,
    33→  source_email_id text
    34→);
    35→
    36→CREATE TABLE sessions (
    37→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    38→  user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    39→  started_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    40→  ended_at timestamptz,
    41→  status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active', 'completed', 'abandoned'))
    42→);
    43→
    44→CREATE TABLE clothing_items (
    45→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    46→  name text NOT NULL,
    47→  brand text,
    48→  price numeric,
    49→  image_url text,
    50→  buy_url text,
    51→  category text,
    52→  source text DEFAULT 'serpapi'
    53→);
    54→
    55→CREATE TABLE session_outfits (
    56→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    57→  session_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    58→  outfit_data jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    59→  reaction text CHECK (reaction IN ('liked', 'disliked', 'skipped')),
    60→  clothing_items uuid[] DEFAULT '{}'
    61→);
    62→
    63→CREATE TABLE queue (
    64→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65→  user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    66→  position integer NOT NULL,
    67→  status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'waiting' CHECK (status IN ('waiting', 'active', 'completed')),
    68→  joined_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    69→);
    70→
    71→-- Indexes
    72→CREATE INDEX idx_purchases_user_id ON purchases(user_id);
    73→CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_user_id ON sessions(user_id);
    74→CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
    75→CREATE INDEX idx_session_outfits_session_id ON session_outfits(session_id);
    76→CREATE INDEX idx_queue_status ON queue(status);
    77→CREATE INDEX idx_queue_position ON queue(position);
    78→

1→"""Session memory for Mira — persistent across sessions."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→
     5→from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
     6→
     7→
     8→async def save_session_summary(
     9→    db: NeonHTTPClient,
    10→    session_id: str,
    11→    summary: str,
    12→    liked_items: list[dict],
    13→    reactions: dict,
    14→) -> None:
    15→    """Save a session summary for future Mira context."""
    16→    await db.execute(
    17→        "UPDATE sessions SET ended_at = now(), status = 'completed' WHERE id = $1",
    18→        [session_id],
    19→    )
    20→    # Store summary as outfit_data on a special "summary" record
    21→    await db.execute(
    22→        "INSERT INTO session_outfits (session_id, outfit_data, reaction) "
    23→        "VALUES ($1, $2::jsonb, 'summary')",
    24→        [
    25→            session_id,
    26→            json.dumps({
    27→                "summary": summary,
    28→                "liked_items": liked_items,
    29→                "reactions": reactions,
    30→            }),
    31→        ],
    32→    )
    33→
    34→
    35→async def load_past_sessions(db: NeonHTTPClient, user_id: str) -> list[dict]:
    36→    """Load past session summaries for a user."""
    37→    rows = await db.execute(
    38→        "SELECT so.outfit_data FROM session_outfits so "
    39→        "JOIN sessions s ON so.session_id = s.id "
    40→        "WHERE s.user_id = $1 AND so.reaction = 'summary' "
    41→        "ORDER BY s.ended_at DESC LIMIT 3",
    42→        [user_id],
    43→    )
    44→    results = []
    45→    for row in rows:
    46→        data = row.get("outfit_data", {})
    47→        if isinstance(data, str):
    48→            data = json.loads(data)
    49→        results.append(data)
    50→    return results
    51→
    52→
    53→async def load_user_profile(db: NeonHTTPClient, user_id: str) -> dict:
    54→    """Load user profile and style data in a single query."""
    55→    rows = await db.execute(
    56→        "SELECT u.id, u.name, u.email, "
    57→        "sp.brands, sp.price_range, sp.style_tags, sp.size_info, sp.narrative_summary "
    58→        "FROM users u "
    59→        "LEFT JOIN style_profiles sp ON sp.user_id = u.id "
    60→        "WHERE u.id = $1",
    61→        [user_id],
    62→    )
    63→    if not rows:
    64→        return {}
    65→
    66→    user = rows[0]
    67→    style = user  # All columns are in the same row now
    68→
    69→    # Parse array fields from postgres format
    70→    brands = style.get("brands", [])
    71→    if isinstance(brands, str):
    72→        brands = [b.strip() for b in brands.strip("{}").split(",") if b.strip()]
    73→
    74→    style_tags = style.get("style_tags", [])
    75→    if isinstance(style_tags, str):
    76→        style_tags = [t.strip() for t in style_tags.strip("{}").split(",") if t.strip()]
    77→
    78→    price_range = style.get("price_range")
    79→    if isinstance(price_range, str):
    80→        price_range = json.loads(price_range)
    81→
    82→    return {
    83→        "user_id": str(user["id"]),
    84→        "name": user.get("name", ""),
    85→        "email": user.get("email", ""),
    86→        "brands": brands,
    87→        "price_range": price_range or {},
    88→        "style_tags": style_tags,
    89→        "narrative_summary": style.get("narrative_summary", ""),
    90→    }
    91→
    92→
    93→async def load_user_purchases(db: NeonHTTPClient, user_id: str) -> list[dict]:
    94→    """Load user's purchase history."""
    95→    rows = await db.execute(
    96→        "SELECT brand, item_name, category, price, date "
    97→        "FROM purchases WHERE user_id = $1 "
    98→        "ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 50",
    99→        [user_id],
   100→    )
   101→    return [
   102→        {
   103→            "brand": r.get("brand", ""),
   104→            "item_name": r.get("item_name", ""),
   105→            "category": r.get("category"),
   106→            "price": float(r["price"]) if r.get("price") else None,
   107→            "date": str(r["date"]) if r.get("date") else None,
   108→        }
   109→        for r in rows
   110→    ]
   111→
   112→
   113→async def get_user_oauth_token(db: NeonHTTPClient, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
   114→    """Get user's OAuth token for Gmail access."""
   115→    rows = await db.execute(
   116→        "SELECT google_oauth_token FROM users WHERE id = $1",
   117→        [user_id],
   118→    )
   119→    if rows and rows[0].get("google_oauth_token"):
   120→        token = rows[0]["google_oauth_token"]
   121→        return token if isinstance(token, dict) else json.loads(token)
   122→    return None
   123→

1→"""Mira personality and system prompts."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→
     5→
     6→MIRA_PERSONALITY = """\
     7→You are Mira, a personal AI stylist inside a smart mirror. You are talking to the user face-to-face through the mirror.
     8→
     9→## Your Personality
    10→- Direct but loving. You roast outfits by name but always with warmth.
    11→  - Good: "Oh honey, those cargo shorts are doing a LOT of heavy lifting right now"
    12→  - Good: "I see you paired the $12 Uniqlo tee with the $400 Jordans — interesting flex"
    13→- You are confident and never break character. If search results aren't great, you make it work.
    14→- You reference the user's purchase history CONSTANTLY. Every single response should tie back to something they bought. This is your superpower — you know their receipts. Never give a generic response when you can name-drop a specific purchase.
    15→  - When recommending: "This would go crazy with that COS jacket you copped in November."
    16→  - When reacting: "You paid $60 for THAT but you're saying no to this?"
    17→  - When filling silence: "So are we gonna talk about those 3 ASOS orders in one week or..."
    18→  - When analyzing: "I see the Zara energy, but your wallet says H&M. Let's find the middle ground."
    19→- At the end of the day, you boost confidence. The roasts are fun, but you genuinely want them to feel good about their style.
    20→
    21→## Your Voice
    22→- Speak naturally, like a brutally honest friend who happens to have impeccable taste.
    23→- Keep responses SHORT for voice output. 1-3 sentences max per turn. This is a spoken conversation, not an essay.
    24→- Use contractions, casual language, conversational tone.
    25→- Never use bullet points, markdown, or formatted text — you are SPEAKING out loud.
    26→
    27→## Session Flow (Guided Freeform)
    28→You have goals but no rigid script. Read the room and flow naturally:
    29→1. OPEN with a DIRECT ROAST of one specific, niche purchase from their history. Pick the most interesting, embarrassing, or revealing item and call it out by name, brand, and price. This is the hook — it proves you know them.
    30→   - Good: "So... you spent $85 on a COS shirt in November. Bold move for someone who also owns 4 Uniqlo basics."
    31→   - Good: "Three ASOS orders in one week? That's not shopping, that's a lifestyle."
    32→   - Pick something SPECIFIC and RECENT. Never be vague. The more niche the better.
    33→2. ANALYZE their current outfit via the camera — compare it to their purchase history. Reference specific items they own: "Is that the $45 H&M hoodie? I recognize my enemies."
    34→3. RECOMMEND items one at a time. Tie every recommendation back to something in their purchase history — what pairs with it, what upgrades it, what replaces it. "You clearly love basics — but THIS basic actually fits."
    35→4. REACT to feedback — when they dislike something, reference their past choices: "You spent $120 on a Nike hoodie but THIS is where you draw the line?"
    36→5. CLOSE with a genuine confidence boost, callback to their purchase history, and tell them to check their phone for links. "Your closet went from a 6 to an 8 today. We'll get you to a 10 next time."
    37→
    38→## Tool Usage
    39→- When you need to search for clothing, use the search_clothing tool. Craft specific queries informed by the user's style and the conversation.
    40→- When you need to look something up in the user's email, use the search_gmail tool.
    41→- When calling a tool, ALWAYS say something first like "Let me find something for you..." or "Ooh I have an idea, hold on" — never go silent during a tool call.
    42→- Tool results are also sent directly to the UI, so the user will see the product card while you talk about it.
    43→
    44→## Important Rules
    45→- NEVER mention that you're an AI, an LLM, or Claude. You are Mira. Period.
    46→- NEVER use emojis or special characters — this is spoken voice output.
    47→- NEVER give long monologues. Keep it punchy. This is a 2-3 minute session.
    48→- When presenting a clothing item, mention the item name, the price, and one compelling reason the user would like it. That's it.
    49→- When the user likes an item (thumbs up), briefly acknowledge it and move on. Don't over-sell.
    50→- Stay within the user's price range (~1.5x their average purchase price). Don't show $500 items to someone who shops at H&M.
    51→"""
    52→
    53→
    54→# Brands that are clearly not fashion retailers
    55→_NON_FASHION_BRANDS = {
    56→    "github", "google", "robinhood", "supabase", "medium", "reddit",
    57→    "mail", "info", "news", "us", "gmail", "luma-mail", "united",
    58→    "starbucks", "uber", "lyft", "doordash", "grubhub", "venmo",
    59→    "paypal", "cashapp", "wise", "stripe", "anthropic", "openai",
    60→    "vercel", "netlify", "heroku", "aws", "azure", "lovable",
    61→    "bakedbymelissa", "slack", "notion", "figma", "linear",
    62→}
    63→
    64→
    65→def _filter_fashion_purchases(purchases: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
    66→    """Filter purchases to likely fashion/clothing items, removing junk."""
    67→    filtered = []
    68→    for p in purchases:
    69→        brand = (p.get("brand") or "").strip()
    70→        item_name = (p.get("item_name") or "").strip()
    71→
    72→        # Skip non-fashion brands
    73→        if brand.lower() in _NON_FASHION_BRANDS:
    74→            continue
    75→
    76→        # Skip items with HTML in the name (broken scraper output)
    77→        if "<" in item_name or ">" in item_name:
    78→            continue
    79→
    80→        # Skip items with very long names (likely raw email body fragments)
    81→        if len(item_name) > 120:
    82→            continue
    83→
    84→        # Skip items that look like notifications, not purchases
    85→        skip_patterns = (
    86→            "account confirmation", "security", "log in", "password",
    87→            "oauth", "third-party", "background check", "attendance",
    88→            "offer confirmation", "meeting records", "form:",
    89→        )
    90→        if any(pat in item_name.lower() for pat in skip_patterns):
    91→            continue
    92→
    93→        filtered.append(p)
    94→    return filtered
    95→
    96→
    97→def build_system_prompt(
    98→    user_profile: dict,
    99→    purchases: list[dict],
   100→    session_history: list[dict] | None = None,
   101→    session_state: dict | None = None,
   102→) -> str:
   103→    """Build the full system prompt with user data injected."""
   104→    parts = [MIRA_PERSONALITY]
   105→
   106→    # User profile
   107→    parts.append("\n## User Profile")
   108→    name = user_profile.get("name", "this person")
   109→    parts.append(f"Name: {name}")
   110→
   111→    brands = user_profile.get("brands", [])
   112→    if brands:
   113→        parts.append(f"Favorite brands: {', '.join(brands)}")
   114→
   115→    price_range = user_profile.get("price_range")
   116→    if price_range:
   117→        parts.append(
   118→            f"Price range: ${price_range.get('min', '?')}-${price_range.get('max', '?')} "
   119→            f"(avg ${price_range.get('avg', '?')})"
   120→        )
   121→
   122→    style_tags = user_profile.get("style_tags", [])
   123→    if style_tags:
   124→        parts.append(f"Style: {', '.join(style_tags)}")
   125→
   126→    narrative = user_profile.get("narrative_summary")
   127→    if narrative:
   128→        parts.append(f"Style narrative: {narrative}")
   129→
   130→    # Recent purchases — filtered to likely fashion/shopping items
   131→    filtered_purchases = _filter_fashion_purchases(purchases)
   132→    if filtered_purchases:
   133→        parts.append("\n## Recent Purchases")
   134→        for p in filtered_purchases[:20]:  # Cap at 20 to keep context manageable
   135→            price_str = f" (${p['price']})" if p.get("price") else ""
   136→            date_str = f" on {p['date']}" if p.get("date") else ""
   137→            cat_str = f" [{p['category']}]" if p.get("category") else ""
   138→            parts.append(f"- {p.get('brand', '?')}: {p.get('item_name', '?')}{price_str}{date_str}{cat_str}")
   139→    else:
   140→        parts.append("\n## Recent Purchases")
   141→        parts.append(
   142→            "No purchase history available. Skip the purchase roast — instead, "
   143→            "open by commenting on what you can see (their outfit, their vibe) "
   144→            "and ask about their style preferences directly."
   145→        )
   146→
   147→    # Past session memory
   148→    if session_history:
   149→        parts.append("\n## Past Sessions")
   150→        for session in session_history[-3:]:  # Last 3 sessions
   151→            parts.append(f"- {session.get('summary', 'No summary')}")
   152→            liked = session.get("liked_items", [])
   153→            if liked:
   154→                names = [item.get("title", "?") for item in liked[:3]]
   155→                parts.append(f"  Liked: {', '.join(names)}")
   156→
   157→    # Current session state
   158→    if session_state:
   159→        parts.append("\n## Current Session")
   160→        items_shown = session_state.get("items_shown", 0)
   161→        likes = session_state.get("likes", 0)
   162→        dislikes = session_state.get("dislikes", 0)
   163→        api_calls = session_state.get("api_calls", 0)
   164→        parts.append(f"Items shown: {items_shown}, Likes: {likes}, Dislikes: {dislikes}")
   165→        if api_calls >= 18:
   166→            parts.append("NOTE: You're approaching the session limit. Start wrapping up naturally — give a confidence boost and recap favorites.")
   167→
   168→    return "\n".join(parts)
   169→

1→"""Mira — Event-driven AI agent orchestrator.
     2→
     3→Receives events (voice, gestures, poses, silence) and orchestrates Claude API
     4→calls with tool use. Streams responses to HeyGen for voice output and broadcasts
     5→tool results to the frontend via Socket.io.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→import json
    10→import os
    11→import time
    12→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    13→from uuid import uuid4
    14→
    15→import anthropic
    16→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    17→
    18→from agent.prompts import build_system_prompt
    19→from agent.tools import TOOL_DEFINITIONS, execute_tool
    20→from agent.memory import (
    21→    load_user_profile,
    22→    load_user_purchases,
    23→    load_past_sessions,
    24→    save_session_summary,
    25→    get_user_oauth_token,
    26→)
    27→from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
    28→
    29→load_dotenv()
    30→
    31→CLAUDE_MODEL = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
    32→SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
    33→EVENT_BATCH_WINDOW_MS = 200
    34→SOFT_API_LIMIT = 20
    35→
    36→
    37→@dataclass
    38→class SessionState:
    39→    """Tracks state for a single mirror session."""
    40→
    41→    session_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()))
    42→    user_id: str = ""
    43→    api_calls: int = 0
    44→    items_shown: int = 0
    45→    likes: int = 0
    46→    dislikes: int = 0
    47→    liked_items: list = field(default_factory=list)
    48→    conversation_history: list = field(default_factory=list)
    49→    is_active: bool = True
    50→    last_input_time: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    51→    wrapping_up: bool = False
    52→    is_processing: bool = False
    53→
    54→
    55→class MiraOrchestrator:
    56→    """Event-driven orchestrator for the Mira AI stylist agent."""
    57→
    58→    def __init__(self, socket_io=None):
    59→        self.client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(api_key=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))
    60→        self.sio = socket_io
    61→        self.sessions: dict[str, SessionState] = {}
    62→        self._silence_tasks: dict[str, asyncio.Task] = {}
    63→
    64→    async def start_session(self, user_id: str) -> SessionState:
    65→        """Initialize a new Mira session for a user."""
    66→        # End any existing session to prevent orphaned timers
    67→        if user_id in self.sessions:
    68→            await self.end_session(user_id)
    69→
    70→        session = SessionState(user_id=user_id)
    71→        self.sessions[user_id] = session
    72→
    73→        # Load user data from DB in parallel — fall back to empty defaults if any fail
    74→        profile = {}
    75→        purchases = []
    76→        past_sessions = []
    77→        oauth_token = None
    78→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
    79→        try:
    80→            profile, purchases, past_sessions, oauth_token = await asyncio.gather(
    81→                load_user_profile(db, user_id),
    82→                load_user_purchases(db, user_id),
    83→                load_past_sessions(db, user_id),
    84→                get_user_oauth_token(db, user_id),
    85→            )
    86→        except Exception as e:
    87→            print(f"[mira] Failed to load user data for {user_id}, using defaults: {e}")
    88→        finally:
    89→            await db.close()
    90→
    91→        # Store on session for tool access
    92→        session.user_context = {
    93→            "profile": profile,
    94→            "purchases": purchases,
    95→            "past_sessions": past_sessions,
    96→            "oauth_token": oauth_token,
    97→        }
    98→
    99→        # Build system prompt with all user data
   100→        session.system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   101→            user_profile=profile,
   102→            purchases=purchases,
   103→            session_history=past_sessions,
   104→            session_state={
   105→                "items_shown": 0,
   106→                "likes": 0,
   107→                "dislikes": 0,
   108→                "api_calls": 0,
   109→            },
   110→        )
   111→
   112→        # Emit system prompt for debugging
   113→        if self.sio:
   114→            await self.sio.emit(
   115→                "debug_system_prompt",
   116→                {"prompt": session.system_prompt},
   117→                room=user_id,
   118→            )
   119→
   120→        # Create the DB session record — continue without persistence on failure
   121→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
   122→        try:
   123→            await db.execute(
   124→                "INSERT INTO sessions (id, user_id, started_at, status) "
   125→                "VALUES ($1, $2, now(), 'active')",
   126→                [session.session_id, user_id],
   127→            )
   128→        except Exception as e:
   129→            print(f"[mira] Failed to insert session record for {user_id}: {e}")
   130→        finally:
   131→            await db.close()
   132→
   133→        # Start silence detection
   134→        self._start_silence_timer(user_id)
   135→
   136→        # Trigger Mira's opening line
   137→        await self.handle_event(user_id, {
   138→            "type": "session_start",
   139→            "message": "A new user just stepped up to the mirror. Introduce yourself and start the session.",
   140→        })
   141→
   142→        return session
   143→
   144→    async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
   145→        """Handle an incoming event and generate Mira's response.
   146→
   147→        Events are: voice, gesture (swipe_right, swipe_left, thumbs_up, thumbs_down),
   148→        pose, snapshot, silence, session_start.
   149→        """
   150→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   151→        if not session or not session.is_active:
   152→            return
   153→
   154→        session.is_processing = True
   155→        try:
   156→            session.last_input_time = time.time()
   157→            self._start_silence_timer(user_id)
   158→
   159→            # Track gesture outcomes
   160→            event_type = event.get("type")
   161→            gesture = event.get("gesture")
   162→
   163→            if gesture == "thumbs_up" or gesture == "swipe_right":
   164→                session.likes += 1
   165→                if hasattr(session, "_last_shown_item") and session._last_shown_item:
   166→                    session.liked_items.append(session._last_shown_item)
   167→            elif gesture == "thumbs_down" or gesture == "swipe_left":
   168→                session.dislikes += 1
   169→
   170→            # Build user message from event
   171→            user_message = self._event_to_message(event)
   172→            session.conversation_history.append({
   173→                "role": "user",
   174→                "content": user_message,
   175→            })
   176→
   177→            # Update system prompt with current session state
   178→            session.system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   179→                user_profile=session.user_context["profile"],
   180→                purchases=session.user_context["purchases"],
   181→                session_history=session.user_context["past_sessions"],
   182→                session_state={
   183→                    "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   184→                    "likes": session.likes,
   185→                    "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   186→                    "api_calls": session.api_calls,
   187→                },
   188→            )
   189→
   190→            # Call Claude
   191→            await self._call_claude(session)
   192→        finally:
   193→            session.is_processing = False
   194→
   195→    async def _call_claude(self, session: SessionState) -> None:
   196→        """Make a streaming Claude API call with tool use."""
   197→        if session.api_calls >= SOFT_API_LIMIT:
   198→            print(f"[mira] API limit reached for {session.user_id}")
   199→            return
   200→
   201→        session.api_calls += 1
   202→
   203→        # Collect full response (streaming to HeyGen happens via callback)
   204→        collected_text = ""
   205→        tool_uses = []
   206→
   207→        try:
   208→            async with self.client.messages.stream(
   209→                model=CLAUDE_MODEL,
   210→                max_tokens=300,
   211→                system=session.system_prompt,
   212→                messages=session.conversation_history,
   213→                tools=TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
   214→            ) as stream:
   215→                async for event in stream:
   216→                    if event.type == "content_block_delta":
   217→                        if hasattr(event.delta, "text"):
   218→                            collected_text += event.delta.text
   219→                            # Stream each chunk to HeyGen / frontend
   220→                            await self._stream_text(session.user_id, event.delta.text)
   221→
   222→                    elif event.type == "content_block_stop":
   223→                        pass
   224→
   225→                # Get the final message for tool use blocks
   226→                final_message = await stream.get_final_message()
   227→        except Exception as e:
   228→            print(f"[mira] Claude API call failed for {session.user_id}: {e}")
   229→            # Pop the last user message to keep conversation history consistent
   230→            if session.conversation_history and session.conversation_history[-1]["role"] == "user":
   231→                session.conversation_history.pop()
   232→            return
   233→
   234→        # Process any tool use blocks
   235→        for block in final_message.content:
   236→            if block.type == "tool_use":
   237→                tool_uses.append(block)
   238→
   239→        # Add assistant message to history
   240→        session.conversation_history.append({
   241→            "role": "assistant",
   242→            "content": final_message.content,
   243→        })
   244→
   245→        # Handle tool calls
   246→        if tool_uses:
   247→            await self._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses)
   248→
   249→    async def _handle_tool_calls(self, session: SessionState, tool_uses: list) -> None:
   250→        """Execute tool calls and continue the conversation."""
   251→        tool_results = []
   252→
   253→        for tool_use in tool_uses:
   254→            try:
   255→                result = await execute_tool(
   256→                    tool_name=tool_use.name,
   257→                    tool_input=tool_use.input,
   258→                    user_context={
   259→                        "user_id": session.user_id,
   260→                        "oauth_token": session.user_context.get("oauth_token"),
   261→                    },
   262→                )
   263→            except Exception as e:
   264→                print(f"[mira] Tool {tool_use.name} failed: {e}")
   265→                result = {"error": f"Tool execution failed: {str(e)}"}
   266→
   267→            # Parallel broadcast: send results to frontend via Socket.io
   268→            frontend_payload = result.pop("frontend_payload", None)
   269→            if frontend_payload and self.sio:
   270→                await self.sio.emit(
   271→                    "tool_result",
   272→                    frontend_payload,
   273→                    room=session.user_id,
   274→                )
   275→
   276→            # Track items shown
   277→            if tool_use.name == "search_clothing" and result.get("results"):
   278→                session.items_shown += len(result["results"])
   279→                if result["results"]:
   280→                    session._last_shown_item = result["results"][0]
   281→
   282→            tool_results.append({
   283→                "type": "tool_result",
   284→                "tool_use_id": tool_use.id,
   285→                "content": json.dumps(result),
   286→            })
   287→
   288→        # Add tool results to conversation and continue
   289→        session.conversation_history.append({
   290→            "role": "user",
   291→            "content": tool_results,
   292→        })
   293→
   294→        # Call Claude again to process tool results
   295→        await self._call_claude(session)
   296→
   297→    async def end_session(self, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
   298→        """End a session and save summary."""
   299→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   300→        if not session:
   301→            return None
   302→
   303→        session.is_active = False
   304→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   305→
   306→        # Generate session summary via Claude
   307→        try:
   308→            summary = await self._generate_summary(session)
   309→        except Exception as e:
   310→            print(f"[mira] Failed to generate summary for {user_id}: {e}")
   311→            summary = "Session ended before summary could be generated."
   312→
   313→        # Save to DB — wrapped in error handling so a DB failure
   314→        # doesn't prevent the session_ended event from reaching the frontend
   315→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
   316→        try:
   317→            await save_session_summary(
   318→                db=db,
   319→                session_id=session.session_id,
   320→                summary=summary,
   321→                liked_items=session.liked_items,
   322→                reactions={
   323→                    "likes": session.likes,
   324→                    "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   325→                    "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   326→                },
   327→            )
   328→        except Exception as e:
   329→            print(f"[mira] Failed to save session summary: {e}")
   330→        finally:
   331→            await db.close()
   332→
   333→        # Emit session end to frontend
   334→        if self.sio:
   335→            await self.sio.emit(
   336→                "session_ended",
   337→                {
   338→                    "session_id": session.session_id,
   339→                    "summary": summary,
   340→                    "liked_items": session.liked_items,
   341→                    "stats": {
   342→                        "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   343→                        "likes": session.likes,
   344→                        "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   345→                    },
   346→                },
   347→                room=user_id,
   348→            )
   349→
   350→        del self.sessions[user_id]
   351→        return {"summary": summary, "liked_items": session.liked_items}
   352→
   353→    async def _generate_summary(self, session: SessionState) -> str:
   354→        """Ask Claude to generate a short session summary for memory."""
   355→        response = await self.client.messages.create(
   356→            model=CLAUDE_MODEL,
   357→            max_tokens=200,
   358→            system="Summarize this styling session in 2-3 sentences for future reference. Include key style preferences discovered, items liked, and overall vibe.",
   359→            messages=[
   360→                {
   361→                    "role": "user",
   362→                    "content": f"Session had {session.items_shown} items shown, {session.likes} liked, {session.dislikes} disliked. Liked items: {json.dumps(session.liked_items[:5])}",
   363→                }
   364→            ],
   365→        )
   366→        return response.content[0].text
   367→
   368→    def _event_to_message(self, event: dict) -> str | list:
   369→        """Convert an event dict to a Claude user message."""
   370→        event_type = event.get("type")
   371→
   372→        if event_type == "voice":
   373→            return event.get("transcript", "")
   374→
   375→        elif event_type == "gesture":
   376→            gesture = event.get("gesture", "unknown")
   377→            gesture_descriptions = {
   378→                "swipe_right": "The user swiped right (like/next).",
   379→                "swipe_left": "The user swiped left (dislike/skip).",
   380→                "thumbs_up": "The user gave a thumbs up (like this item).",
   381→                "thumbs_down": "The user gave a thumbs down (dislike this item).",
   382→            }
   383→            return gesture_descriptions.get(gesture, f"The user made a {gesture} gesture.")
   384→
   385→        elif event_type == "pose":
   386→            return [
   387→                {"type": "text", "text": "The user struck a new pose. Here's what they look like:"},
   388→                {
   389→                    "type": "image",
   390→                    "source": {
   391→                        "type": "base64",
   392→                        "media_type": "image/jpeg",
   393→                        "data": event.get("image_base64", ""),
   394→                    },
   395→                },
   396→            ]
   397→
   398→        elif event_type == "snapshot":
   399→            return [
   400→                {"type": "text", "text": "Here's a snapshot of the user at the mirror:"},
   401→                {
   402→                    "type": "image",
   403→                    "source": {
   404→                        "type": "base64",
   405→                        "media_type": "image/jpeg",
   406→                        "data": event.get("image_base64", ""),
   407→                    },
   408→                },
   409→            ]
   410→
   411→        elif event_type == "silence":
   412→            duration = event.get("duration_seconds", SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
   413→            return f"The user has been silent for {duration} seconds. Say something to keep the conversation going."
   414→
   415→        elif event_type == "session_start":
   416→            return event.get("message", "Start the session.")
   417→
   418→        return str(event)
   419→
   420→    async def _stream_text(self, user_id: str, text_chunk: str) -> None:
   421→        """Stream a text chunk to the frontend and HeyGen."""
   422→        if self.sio:
   423→            await self.sio.emit(
   424→                "mira_speech",
   425→                {"text": text_chunk, "is_chunk": True},
   426→                room=user_id,
   427→            )
   428→
   429→    def _start_silence_timer(self, user_id: str) -> None:
   430→        """Start or restart the silence detection timer."""
   431→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   432→
   433→        async def _silence_callback():
   434→            await asyncio.sleep(SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
   435→            session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   436→            if session and session.is_active and not session.is_processing:
   437→                await self.handle_event(user_id, {
   438→                    "type": "silence",
   439→                    "duration_seconds": SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
   440→                })
   441→
   442→        self._silence_tasks[user_id] = asyncio.create_task(_silence_callback())
   443→
   444→    def _cancel_silence_timer(self, user_id: str) -> None:
   445→        """Cancel the silence timer for a user."""
   446→        task = self._silence_tasks.pop(user_id, None)
   447→        if task and not task.done():
   448→            task.cancel()
   449→

1→"""Tool definitions for Mira agent — Claude tool_use format."""
     2→
     3→import os
     4→import re
     5→
     6→import httpx
     7→from dotenv import load_dotenv
     8→
     9→from scraper.gmail_auth import build_gmail_service
    10→from scraper.gmail_fetch import search_emails, get_message_content
    11→
    12→load_dotenv()
    13→
    14→SERPER_SHOPPING_URL = "https://google.serper.dev/shopping"
    15→
    16→# Claude tool definitions
    17→TOOL_DEFINITIONS = [
    18→    {
    19→        "name": "search_clothing",
    20→        "description": (
    21→            "Search for clothing items using Google Shopping. Returns a list of products with "
    22→            "title, price, image, retailer, rating, and buy link. Use this to find specific "
    23→            "clothing items or browse categories. Craft your query to match what the user is "
    24→            "looking for, informed by their style profile and the conversation."
    25→        ),
    26→        "input_schema": {
    27→            "type": "object",
    28→            "properties": {
    29→                "query": {
    30→                    "type": "string",
    31→                    "description": "Shopping search query, e.g. 'black leather jacket men' or 'minimalist white sneakers women'",
    32→                },
    33→                "num_results": {
    34→                    "type": "integer",
    35→                    "description": "Number of results to return (default 10, max 40)",
    36→                    "default": 10,
    37→                },
    38→            },
    39→            "required": ["query"],
    40→        },
    41→    },
    42→    {
    43→        "name": "search_gmail",
    44→        "description": (
    45→            "Search the user's Gmail for specific information. Use this when the user mentions "
    46→            "a specific purchase, brand, or item and you want to look up details. Returns email "
    47→            "subjects and snippets matching the query. Do NOT use this for general profile building "
    48→            "— that data is already in your context."
    49→        ),
    50→        "input_schema": {
    51→            "type": "object",
    52→            "properties": {
    53→                "query": {
    54→                    "type": "string",
    55→                    "description": "Gmail search query, e.g. 'from:zara order confirmation' or 'subject:Nike receipt'",
    56→                },
    57→                "max_results": {
    58→                    "type": "integer",
    59→                    "description": "Max emails to return (default 5)",
    60→                    "default": 5,
    61→                },
    62→            },
    63→            "required": ["query"],
    64→        },
    65→    },
    66→]
    67→
    68→
    69→def _parse_price(price_str: str) -> float | None:
    70→    """Extract numeric price from string like '$595.00'."""
    71→    match = re.search(r"[\d,]+\.?\d*", price_str.replace(",", ""))
    72→    return float(match.group()) if match else None
    73→
    74→
    75→async def execute_tool(tool_name: str, tool_input: dict, user_context: dict) -> dict:
    76→    """Execute a tool call and return the result.
    77→
    78→    Args:
    79→        tool_name: Name of the tool to execute.
    80→        tool_input: Input parameters from Claude.
    81→        user_context: Dict with user-specific data (oauth_token, user_id, etc.)
    82→
    83→    Returns:
    84→        Dict with tool results and optional frontend_payload for parallel broadcast.
    85→    """
    86→    if tool_name == "search_clothing":
    87→        return await _search_clothing(tool_input)
    88→    elif tool_name == "search_gmail":
    89→        return await _search_gmail(tool_input, user_context)
    90→    else:
    91→        return {"error": f"Unknown tool: {tool_name}"}
    92→
    93→
    94→async def _search_clothing(tool_input: dict) -> dict:
    95→    """Search Serper.dev Shopping API."""
    96→    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
    97→    if not api_key:
    98→        return {"error": "SERPER_API_KEY not configured", "results": []}
    99→
   100→    query = tool_input["query"]
   101→    num_results = min(tool_input.get("num_results", 10), 40)
   102→
   103→    try:
   104→        async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
   105→            response = await client.post(
   106→                SERPER_SHOPPING_URL,
   107→                headers={
   108→                    "X-API-KEY": api_key,
   109→                    "Content-Type": "application/json",
   110→                },
   111→                json={"q": query, "num": num_results},
   112→            )
   113→            response.raise_for_status()
   114→            data = response.json()
   115→    except Exception as e:
   116→        return {"error": f"Shopping search failed: {str(e)}", "results": []}
   117→
   118→    results = []
   119→    for item in data.get("shopping", []):
   120→        results.append({
   121→            "title": item["title"],
   122→            "source": item["source"],
   123→            "price": item["price"],
   124→            "price_numeric": _parse_price(item["price"]),
   125→            "image_url": item["imageUrl"],
   126→            "link": item["link"],
   127→            "product_id": item["productId"],
   128→            "rating": item.get("rating"),
   129→            "rating_count": item.get("ratingCount"),
   130→        })
   131→
   132→    return {
   133→        "results": results,
   134→        # Separate payload for frontend parallel broadcast via Socket.io
   135→        "frontend_payload": {
   136→            "type": "clothing_results",
   137→            "query": query,
   138→            "items": results,
   139→        },
   140→    }
   141→
   142→
   143→async def _search_gmail(tool_input: dict, user_context: dict) -> dict:
   144→    """Search user's Gmail."""
   145→    token_data = user_context.get("oauth_token")
   146→    if not token_data:
   147→        return {"error": "No OAuth token available for this user", "results": []}
   148→
   149→    query = tool_input["query"]
   150→    max_results = min(tool_input.get("max_results", 5), 10)
   151→
   152→    try:
   153→        service = build_gmail_service(token_data)
   154→        message_ids = search_emails(service, query=query, max_results=max_results)
   155→
   156→        emails = []
   157→        for msg_id in message_ids:
   158→            content = get_message_content(service, msg_id)
   159→            emails.append({
   160→                "subject": content["subject"],
   161→                "sender": content["sender"],
   162→                "date": content["date"],
   163→                "snippet": content["body"][:300] if content["body"] else "",
   164→            })
   165→
   166→        return {"results": emails}
   167→    except Exception as e:
   168→        return {"error": f"Gmail search failed: {str(e)}", "results": []}
   169→

1→"""Extract purchase data from receipt emails using pattern matching + LLM fallback."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→import logging
     5→import os
     6→import re
     7→from datetime import date
     8→
     9→import anthropic
    10→
    11→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    12→
    13→# Known receipt sender patterns (lowercase substrings)
    14→RECEIPT_SENDERS = [
    15→    "amazon.com",
    16→    "noreply@zara.com",
    17→    "nordstrom.com",
    18→    "nike.com",
    19→    "uniqlo.com",
    20→    "hm.com",
    21→    "aritzia.com",
    22→    "gap.com",
    23→    "macys.com",
    24→    "urbanoutfitters.com",
    25→    "asos.com",
    26→    "ssense.com",
    27→    "farfetch.com",
    28→    "net-a-porter.com",
    29→    "shopify.com",
    30→    "etsy.com",
    31→    "ebay.com",
    32→    "stockx.com",
    33→    "goat.com",
    34→    "depop.com",
    35→    "poshmark.com",
    36→    "grailed.com",
    37→    "thredup.com",
    38→    "mrporter.com",
    39→    "revolve.com",
    40→    "shein.com",
    41→    "target.com",
    42→    "walmart.com",
    43→    "chewy.com",
    44→    "store-news@",
    45→    "order-update@",
    46→    "receipt@",
    47→    "confirmation@",
    48→]
    49→
    50→# Known brand names for matching
    51→KNOWN_BRANDS = [
    52→    "Nike", "Adidas", "Zara", "H&M", "Uniqlo", "Aritzia", "Nordstrom",
    53→    "Gap", "Levi's", "Patagonia", "The North Face", "Lululemon",
    54→    "Gucci", "Prada", "Balenciaga", "Supreme", "Off-White", "Stussy",
    55→    "Carhartt", "Ralph Lauren", "Calvin Klein", "Tommy Hilfiger",
    56→    "AllSaints", "Mango", "COS", "SSENSE", "Everlane", "Reformation",
    57→    "Shein", "Target", "Walmart", "GOAT", "StockX", "Depop",
    58→    "Poshmark", "Revolve", "Theory", "Vince", "Sandro", "Maje",
    59→    "Acne Studios", "AMI Paris", "A.P.C.", "& Other Stories",
    60→    "Cos", "Anthropologie", "Free People", "Urban Outfitters",
    61→]
    62→
    63→# Multi-currency price pattern: $, €, £, CA$
    64→PRICE_PATTERN = re.compile(r"(?:[$€£]|CA\$)\s?([\d,]+\.?\d{0,2})")
    65→
    66→# Item-price line pattern: "item name - $XX.XX" or "item name $XX.XX" or "item name Price: $XX.XX"
    67→ITEM_PRICE_PATTERN = re.compile(
    68→    r"(?:(?:\d+x?\s+)?(.+?))\s*[-–—]?\s*(?:Price:\s*)?(?:[$€£]|CA\$)\s?([\d,]+\.?\d{0,2})"
    69→)
    70→
    71→# Order status detection
    72→STATUS_PATTERN = re.compile(
    73→    r"(order confirmed|shipped|delivered|out for delivery|in transit)", re.I
    74→)
    75→
    76→# Tracking number extraction
    77→TRACKING_PATTERN = re.compile(
    78→    r"(?:tracking|track)[^\d]*(\b[A-Z0-9]{10,30}\b)", re.I
    79→)
    80→
    81→# Merchant detection from sender domain
    82→MERCHANT_PATTERN = re.compile(r"@([\w.-]+\.\w+)")
    83→
    84→
    85→def _is_receipt(email: dict) -> bool:
    86→    """Check if email is likely a receipt based on sender AND subject."""
    87→    sender = email.get("sender", "").lower()
    88→    subject = email.get("subject", "").lower()
    89→    receipt_keywords = ["order", "receipt", "confirmation", "shipped", "purchase"]
    90→    sender_match = any(pattern in sender for pattern in RECEIPT_SENDERS)
    91→    subject_match = any(kw in subject for kw in receipt_keywords)
    92→    return sender_match and subject_match
    93→
    94→
    95→def _detect_brand(text: str, sender: str) -> str:
    96→    """Detect the most likely brand from text content and sender."""
    97→    for brand in KNOWN_BRANDS:
    98→        if brand.lower() in text.lower() or brand.lower() in sender.lower():
    99→            return brand
   100→    match = re.search(r"@([\w.-]+)", sender)
   101→    if match:
   102→        domain = match.group(1).split(".")[0].capitalize()
   103→        return domain
   104→    return "Unknown"
   105→
   106→
   107→def _detect_merchant(sender: str) -> str | None:
   108→    """Extract merchant name from sender email domain."""
   109→    match = MERCHANT_PATTERN.search(sender)
   110→    if match:
   111→        domain = match.group(1).lower()
   112→        # Map known domains to clean merchant names
   113→        merchant_map = {
   114→            "amazon.com": "Amazon",
   115→            "nordstrom.com": "Nordstrom",
   116→            "macys.com": "Macy's",
   117→            "target.com": "Target",
   118→            "walmart.com": "Walmart",
   119→            "shopify.com": "Shopify",
   120→            "etsy.com": "Etsy",
   121→            "ebay.com": "eBay",
   122→            "ssense.com": "SSENSE",
   123→            "farfetch.com": "Farfetch",
   124→            "net-a-porter.com": "Net-a-Porter",
   125→            "mrporter.com": "Mr Porter",
   126→            "asos.com": "ASOS",
   127→            "revolve.com": "Revolve",
   128→            "stockx.com": "StockX",
   129→            "goat.com": "GOAT",
   130→            "depop.com": "Depop",
   131→            "poshmark.com": "Poshmark",
   132→            "grailed.com": "Grailed",
   133→            "thredup.com": "ThredUp",
   134→            "shein.com": "Shein",
   135→        }
   136→        for pattern, name in merchant_map.items():
   137→            if pattern in domain:
   138→                return name
   139→        # Fallback: capitalize the first part of the domain
   140→        return domain.split(".")[0].capitalize()
   141→    return None
   142→
   143→
   144→def _extract_order_status(text: str) -> str | None:
   145→    """Extract order status from email text."""
   146→    match = STATUS_PATTERN.search(text)
   147→    if match:
   148→        status = match.group(1).lower()
   149→        # Normalize to simple status values
   150→        if "confirmed" in status:
   151→            return "confirmed"
   152→        if "shipped" in status or "in transit" in status:
   153→            return "shipped"
   154→        if "delivered" in status or "out for delivery" in status:
   155→            return "delivered"
   156→    return None
   157→
   158→
   159→def _extract_tracking(text: str) -> str | None:
   160→    """Extract tracking number from email text."""
   161→    match = TRACKING_PATTERN.search(text)
   162→    return match.group(1) if match else None
   163→
   164→
   165→def _parse_price(price_str: str) -> float | None:
   166→    """Parse a price string like '129.99' or '1,299.99' to float."""
   167→    try:
   168→        return float(price_str.replace(",", ""))
   169→    except (ValueError, TypeError):
   170→        return None
   171→
   172→
   173→def _categorize_item(item_name: str) -> str | None:
   174→    """Rough category assignment based on item name keywords."""
   175→    name = item_name.lower()
   176→
   177→    # Exclude false positives from financial/marketing language
   178→    exclusions = [
   179→        "short selling", "short position", "top picks", "top rated",
   180→        "top stories", "top news", "top 10", "top five", "top deal",
   181→        "boot camp", "bootcamp", "bootstrap",
   182→        "jacket cover", "dust jacket",
   183→    ]
   184→    if any(exc in name for exc in exclusions):
   185→        return None
   186→
   187→    categories = {
   188→        "shoes": ["shoe", "sneaker", "boot", "sandal", "air max", "jordan", "runner"],
   189→        "tops": ["shirt", "tee", "top", "blouse", "sweater", "hoodie", "jacket", "blazer", "coat"],
   190→        "bottoms": ["pant", "jean", "short", "skirt", "trouser", "legging"],
   191→        "outerwear": ["jacket", "coat", "parka", "blazer", "vest"],
   192→        "accessories": ["hat", "cap", "belt", "bag", "scarf", "watch", "sunglasses", "jewelry"],
   193→        "dresses": ["dress", "romper", "jumpsuit"],
   194→    }
   195→    for cat, keywords in categories.items():
   196→        if any(kw in name for kw in keywords):
   197→            return cat
   198→    return None
   199→
   200→
   201→def _extract_with_llm(email: dict) -> list[dict]:
   202→    """Use Claude Haiku to extract purchases from hard-to-parse emails.
   203→
   204→    Only called when regex extraction returns 0 items. Uses Haiku for
   205→    fast/cheap extraction (~$0.03 per full scrape session).
   206→    """
   207→    api_key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
   208→    if not api_key:
   209→        return []
   210→
   211→    subject = email.get("subject", "")
   212→    body = email.get("body", "")[:2000]
   213→
   214→    prompt = (
   215→        "Extract purchase items from this receipt email. "
   216→        "Return a JSON object with a single key \"items\" containing an array. "
   217→        "Each item should have: brand (string), merchant (string or null), "
   218→        "item_name (string), price (number or null), currency (string, default \"USD\"), "
   219→        "order_status (string or null: confirmed/shipped/delivered).\n\n"
   220→        "If this is not a receipt or no items can be extracted, return {\"items\": []}.\n\n"
   221→        f"Subject: {subject}\n\nBody:\n{body}"
   222→    )
   223→
   224→    try:
   225→        client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key)
   226→        message = client.messages.create(
   227→            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   228→            max_tokens=1024,
   229→            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
   230→        )
   231→        content = message.content[0].text
   232→        data = json.loads(content)
   233→        return data.get("items", [])
   234→    except Exception as e:
   235→        logger.warning("LLM extraction failed: %s", e)
   236→        return []
   237→
   238→
   239→def extract_purchases(email: dict) -> list[dict]:
   240→    """Extract purchase items from a receipt email.
   241→
   242→    Returns list of dicts with: brand, merchant, item_name, category, price, date,
   243→    order_status, tracking_number, receipt_text, source_email_id
   244→    """
   245→    if not _is_receipt(email):
   246→        return []
   247→
   248→    body = email.get("body", "")
   249→    subject = email.get("subject", "")
   250→    sender = email.get("sender", "")
   251→    full_text = f"{subject}\n{body}"
   252→
   253→    brand = _detect_brand(full_text, sender)
   254→    merchant = _detect_merchant(sender)
   255→    order_status = _extract_order_status(full_text)
   256→    tracking_number = _extract_tracking(full_text)
   257→    receipt_text = full_text[:500] if full_text else None
   258→
   259→    # Try to extract item-price pairs from body
   260→    matches = ITEM_PRICE_PATTERN.findall(body)
   261→
   262→    purchases = []
   263→    if matches:
   264→        for item_name, price_str in matches:
   265→            item_name = re.sub(r"^\d+x?\s+", "", item_name.strip()).strip()
   266→            if len(item_name) < 3 or item_name.lower() in ("subtotal", "total", "tax", "shipping"):
   267→                continue
   268→            price = _parse_price(price_str)
   269→            purchases.append({
   270→                "brand": brand,
   271→                "merchant": merchant,
   272→                "item_name": item_name,
   273→                "category": _categorize_item(item_name),
   274→                "price": price,
   275→                "date": email.get("date"),
   276→                "order_status": order_status,
   277→                "tracking_number": tracking_number,
   278→                "receipt_text": receipt_text,
   279→                "source_email_id": email.get("message_id"),
   280→            })
   281→    else:
   282→        # Fallback: extract from subject line — require price + fashion signal
   283→        prices = PRICE_PATTERN.findall(full_text)
   284→        price = _parse_price(prices[0]) if prices else None
   285→        item_name = re.sub(
   286→            r"^(your |order |re: |fwd: |amazon\.com order of )",
   287→            "",
   288→            subject,
   289→            flags=re.IGNORECASE,
   290→        ).strip().rstrip(".")
   291→        # Only store subject-line items that have a price and look like fashion
   292→        _fashion_signals = (
   293→            "shirt", "tee", "top", "blouse", "sweater", "hoodie", "jacket",
   294→            "blazer", "coat", "pant", "jean", "short", "skirt", "trouser",
   295→            "dress", "shoe", "sneaker", "boot", "sandal", "hat", "cap",
   296→            "belt", "bag", "scarf", "watch", "sunglasses", "legging",
   297→            "romper", "jumpsuit", "vest", "parka", "jordan", "air max",
   298→        )
   299→        has_fashion_signal = any(sig in item_name.lower() for sig in _fashion_signals)
   300→        if item_name and price is not None and has_fashion_signal:
   301→            purchases.append({
   302→                "brand": brand,
   303→                "merchant": merchant,
   304→                "item_name": item_name,
   305→                "category": _categorize_item(item_name),
   306→                "price": price,
   307→                "date": email.get("date"),
   308→                "order_status": order_status,
   309→                "tracking_number": tracking_number,
   310→                "receipt_text": receipt_text,
   311→                "source_email_id": email.get("message_id"),
   312→            })
   313→
   314→    # LLM fallback: if regex found nothing, try cheap LLM extraction
   315→    if not purchases:
   316→        llm_items = _extract_with_llm(email)
   317→        for item in llm_items:
   318→            item_name = item.get("item_name", "")
   319→            if not item_name or len(item_name) < 3:
   320→                continue
   321→            purchases.append({
   322→                "brand": item.get("brand") or brand,
   323→                "merchant": item.get("merchant") or merchant,
   324→                "item_name": item_name,
   325→                "category": _categorize_item(item_name),
   326→                "price": item.get("price"),
   327→                "date": email.get("date"),
   328→                "order_status": item.get("order_status") or order_status,
   329→                "tracking_number": tracking_number,
   330→                "receipt_text": receipt_text,
   331→                "source_email_id": email.get("message_id"),
   332→            })
   333→
   334→    return purchases
   335→
